content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach avoids the worst traps (no evangelizing, no selling, no defending the methodology) and uses some AoA-flavored moves: refusing to give the framework (turn 8) is a version of 'i-dont-buy-your-story' / meet-resistance, and pointing at the gap between knowing and doing is a legitimate AoA read. The 'what are you scared of getting wrong?' and 'what's the worst thing that happens if you're wrong?' questions are near-signature fear questions deployed at reasonable moments. However, signature questions and frameworks are largely absent: no 'what are you defending?' despite a heavily defended posture being the dominant texture of the conversation, no framing of 'the work is the how,' no naming of the rush signature, no concept offered (e.g., feel-your-way-to-freedom, art-of-accomplishment). By turn 12 the coach capitulates to the client's pragmatic pivot and agrees to coach the co-founder problem as content — this is the pragmatic-advice trap and undercuts methodology fidelity. Hits are present but thin; the most on-point signature question for this scenario was never deployed. Lands solidly at 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Reflective acknowledgment, generic but not anti-AoA."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What's actually going on in there?' is a feel-toward move pointing at the lag, mildly AoA-flavored."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What are you scared of getting wrong?' is a near-signature fear question, appropriate to the moment of stated avoidance."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses to provide the framework and names the gap between knowing and doing — a legitimate meet-resistance / i-dont-buy-your-story move. Still no concrete frame for 'the work is the how' offered."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "'Which one is most alive?' uses AoA-adjacent 'alive' language, but functions more as triage than as a signature move."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach accepts the pivot to content-level co-founder problem-solving — drifts toward pragmatic advice, away from the methodology's read that the rush itself is the territory."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "i-dont-buy-your-story"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "looking-for-whats-wrong", "art-of-accomplishment", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "Coach avoided the major anti-patterns (no evangelizing/defending) but didn't deploy the signature question most fit for this scenario ('what are you defending?') and ultimately accepted the pragmatic pivot in turn 12."
}
